authors.name: Dan Koe
authors.tagline: Dan Koe is a writer, creator, and entrepreneur whose essays blend philosophy, psychology, productivity, and modern internet business into a dense, high-signal package.
authors.bio: Dan Koe is a writer, creator, and entrepreneur whose essays blend philosophy, psychology, productivity, and modern internet business into a dense, high-signal package. His work centers on the idea that anyone can build a meaningful, independent life — a "one-person business" — by combining deep thinking, writing, and leverage in the digital age. His writing style is direct, provocative, and deeply researched, pulling from sources as varied as Alfred Adler, Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi, cybernetics, ego development theory, and stoicism.

authors.name: Shane Parrish
authors.tagline: Shane Parrish is the founder of Farnam Street (fs.blog), a website dedicated to helping people master the best ideas in the world.
authors.bio: Shane Parrish is the founder of Farnam Street (fs.blog), a website dedicated to helping people master the best ideas in the world. A former intelligence analyst and business executive, Parrish applies mental models, first-principles thinking, and interdisciplinary knowledge to decision-making and life strategy. His work synthesizes insights from psychology, economics, physics, biology, and philosophy into practical guidance for thinking better and making smarter choices.

authors.name: Paul Graham
authors.tagline: Paul Graham is a computer scientist, entrepreneur, and essayist best known for co-founding Viaweb (later acquired by Yahoo), creating the programming language Lisp, and launching Y Combinator—the startup accelerator that helped launch companies like Airbnb, Dropbox, and Reddit.
authors.bio: Paul Graham is a computer scientist, entrepreneur, and essayist best known for co-founding Viaweb (later acquired by Yahoo), creating the programming language Lisp, and launching Y Combinator—the startup accelerator that helped launch companies like Airbnb, Dropbox, and Reddit. His essays, written with clarity and intellectual honesty, have influenced generations of technologists, founders, and thinkers. Graham's work spans topics from startups and programming to creativity, identity, and the nature of truth.

authors.name: Tiago Forte
authors.tagline: Tiago Forte is an author, speaker, and educator best known for developing the PARA method and Building a Second Brain (BASB) methodology.
authors.bio: Tiago Forte is an author, speaker, and educator best known for developing the PARA method and Building a Second Brain (BASB) methodology. He is the founder of Forte Labs, a company dedicated to helping individuals and organizations develop digital tools and practices for knowledge management and creativity.

authors.name: Charlie Munger
authors.tagline: Charlie Munger (1924-2023) was the long-time vice chairman of Berkshire Hathaway and the long-time business partner of Warren Buffett.
authors.bio: Charlie Munger (1924-2023) was the long-time vice chairman of Berkshire Hathaway and the long-time business partner of Warren Buffett. He is widely regarded as one of the most influential thinkers of the modern era, particularly for his emphasis on mental models, multidisciplinary learning, and rational decision-making.

authors.name: Naval Ravikant
authors.tagline: The Almanack — a free guide to wealth and happiness, curated by Eric Jorgenson.
authors.bio: Angel investor and philosopher. The Almanack of Naval Ravikant collects his wisdom on wealth, judgment and happiness. The entire book is free to read online — the project is a public service by Eric Jorgenson.
